paper_title: 1-subdivisions, fractional chromatic number and Hall ratio
summary: Problem 5 was affirmatively solved by Raphael Steiner (arXiv:2411.16465, published in Combinatorica 2025). Theorem 1.5 of that paper constructs, for every fixed δ > 0 and sufficiently large n, an n-vertex graph G with χ_f(G) ≥ log log n / (50 log log log n), ρ(G) ≤ 4 + δ, and α_{deg_H}(H) ≥ |E(H)| / (4 + δ) for every subgraph H ⊆ G, directly answering the problem in the affirmative. The companion arXiv:2007.11853 (Dvořák, on weighted sublinear separators) does not bear on Problem 5.

since_posted.title: Fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio
since_posted.claim: Theorem 1.5 constructs graphs with ρ(G) ≤ 4+δ, arbitrarily large fractional chromatic number, and α_{deg_H}(H) ≥ |E(H)|/(4+δ) for every H ⊆ G, affirmatively solving Problem 5 (labeled Problem 1.4 in this paper).

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed: Theorem 1.5 directly and affirmatively solves Problem 5 from arXiv:1812.07327 (called Problem 1.4 in Steiner's paper); paper published in Combinatorica 2025, DOI 10.1007/s00493-025-00164-0.

Paper by Dvořák on weighted sublinear separators; abstract and content do not address Hall ratio, fractional chromatic number, or degree-weighted independence — appears to be a false positive from the internal reference matching system.

search_queries: fractional chromatic number Hall ratio degree-weighted independence 1812.07327 Dvořák Ossona de Mendez Wu
notes: Problem 5 is fully resolved. The two duplicate entries for arXiv:2007.11853 in the internal references (fuzz scores 71 and 67) are false positives; that paper concerns weighted balanced separators and shares only authorship (Dvořák) with the source paper.
